codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the scoped home/setup shell requirements: the three primary modes are visible, setup state is URL-backed, duration is clamped, and popstate handling restores safe pre-game state without auto-starting play. I found one non-blocking history-management issue, but no blocking correctness gaps for this slice.

issues.description: Clicking the already-selected mode card still calls `patchState({ mode }, 'push')`, which adds duplicate browser-history entries for the same URL/state. That makes Back/Forward navigation noisier than intended at the home/setup level and can require extra Back presses before the user returns to the prior distinct state. Guard against no-op pushes before calling `pushState`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `web-game-worker` skill says to use `agent-browser` for manual verification, but this worker had to fall back to Playwright browser tools after an `agent-browser` socket issue. The skill should explicitly document Playwright browser tools as an accepted fallback when the dedicated browser agent is blocked by local daemon/session problems.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file `.factory/skills/web-game-worker/SKILL.md` step 6 requires `agent-browser`; handoff `skillFeedback.deviations[1]` says the worker used built-in Playwright browser tools instead because the `agent-browser` session could not start reliably due to a stale local socket.

summary: Review passed. The home shell implementation satisfies the feature's scoped requirements, with one non-blocking issue around duplicate history entries when the active mode card is clicked repeatedly.
